entries.title: 3.0L Diesel
entries.history: The ASB was the first V6 TDI fitted to the C6 A6 Allroad, delivering 230bhp with quattro as standard. It established the 3.0 TDI as the Allroad’s dominant powertrain.
compatibleVariants: A6 Allroad 3.0 TDI quattro (C6, pre-facelift), A6 Allroad 3.0 TDI SE, A6 Allroad 3.0 TDI S line
commonFailures: Turbo actuator failure causing limp mode

entries.title: 3.0L Diesel
entries.history: The BHK succeeded the ASB in the C6 facelift, bringing higher power and slightly improved emissions while maintaining the bulletproof V6 TDI architecture.

entries.title: 3.0L Diesel
entries.history: The C7-generation TDI engines introduced ultra-clean emissions and improved efficiency. The CDUC (204hp) and CDYB (245hp) offered two power outputs for the same 3.0 V6 block.
compatibleVariants: A6 Allroad 3.0 TDI (C7), A6 Allroad 3.0 TDI SE, A6 Allroad 3.0 TDI S line, A6 Allroad 3.0 TDI Black Edition
entries.cta: Get quotes for CDUC / CDYB Audi Allroad engine replacement

entries.title: 3.0L Diesel
entries.history: The C7 facelift brought the 218hp CZVA and the highly sought-after 320hp BiTDI (CRTE) with sequential turbocharging. The CRTE remains the most searched Allroad diesel for replacement.
compatibleVariants: A6 Allroad 3.0 TDI (C7 facelift), A6 Allroad 3.0 BiTDI, A6 Allroad Competition, A6 Allroad Vorsprung
commonFailures: Rocker arm failure (ticking noise, internal damage)
HPFP failure sending metal debris through system
entries.cta: Get quotes for CZVA / CRTE Audi Allroad engine replacement

entries.title: 2.0L Diesel
entries.history: The CAEA was Audi’s first common-rail 2.0 TDI for the B8 A4 Allroad, replacing older unit injector engines. It prioritised refinement and fuel economy.
compatibleVariants: A4 Allroad 2.0 TDI (B8), A4 Allroad 2.0 TDI SE
entries.cta: Get quotes for CAEA Audi Allroad engine replacement

entries.title: 2.0L Diesel
entries.history: A higher-output version of the CAEA, the CAGA delivered 170hp, making the A4 Allroad significantly more capable for fully loaded touring.
compatibleVariants: A4 Allroad 2.0 TDI (B8), A4 Allroad 2.0 TDI SE, A4 Allroad 2.0 TDI S line

entries.title: 2.0L Diesel
entries.history: The B8.5 facelift brought these Euro 6-compliant 2.0 TDIs, available in 150hp (CYMC) and 190hp (CYKB) outputs. The 190hp version was the most powerful four-cylinder diesel in the A4 Allroad.
compatibleVariants: A4 Allroad 2.0 TDI (B8.5), A4 Allroad 2.0 TDI ultra, A4 Allroad 2.0 TDI S line

entries.title: 2.5L Diesel
entries.history: The 2.5 TDI V6 was the sole diesel option for the original C5 Allroad. With 180hp and a distinctive V6 rumble, it offered relaxed touring capability and remains popular in the enthusiast classic market.
compatibleVariants: C5 Allroad 2.5 TDI quattro, C5 Allroad 2.5 TDI SE

entries.title: 2.7L Petrol
entries.history: The 2.7T was the original C5 Allroad hero engine - a twin-turbo V6 producing 250hp. Enthusiasts still seek these engines for rebuilds with upgraded turbos, making it a premium specialist market.
compatibleVariants: C5 Allroad 2.7T quattro, C5 Allroad 2.7T SE, C5 Allroad 2.7T S line

commonProblems.h2: Audi Allroad Engine Problems - What It Costs to Fix & When Replacement Makes Sense

problems.h4: 2.0 TFSI Timing Chain Tensioner Failure - Why B8/B9 Allroad Engines Fail Prematurely
problems.affectedModels: Audi A4 Allroad 2.0 TFSI (B8, B8.5, B9; 2008-2020, engine codes CDNC, CGQB, DETA)
problems.rootCause: The original timing chain tensioner design uses a mechanical spring that loses tension over time, allowing the chain to slacken, skip teeth, and ultimately snap. Pre-2012 engines are most vulnerable; post-2012 received an updated tensioner but failures still occur.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace timing chain, updated tensioner, guides, and sprockets before failure occurs
repairOptions.longevity: 60k-80k miles; only viable if caught early with rattle on startup

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt engine with upgraded timing components after chain snap bends valves
repairOptions.longevity: 12-month warranty; only option once chain has snapped

problems.recommendation: If you hear a rattling noise on cold startup for 1-2 seconds, act immediately. A preventative chain replacement at a specialist costs under £1,400 and saves your engine. Once the chain snaps, the engine is scrap - replacement is your only path. On a B8 Allroad worth £4,000-£8,000, preventative work is almost always the better financial decision if caught early.
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt 2.0 TFSI engine prices for your A4 Allroad

problems.h4: 3.0 TDI Injector Failure - The Most Common C6/C7 A6 Allroad Diesel Problem
problems.affectedModels: Audi A6 Allroad 3.0 TDI (C6, C6 facelift, C7; 2006-2014, engine codes ASB, BHK, CDUC, CDYB)
problems.rootCause: Piezo injectors on the 3.0 TDI V6 develop internal leakage or electrical faults, causing misfires, rough idling, excessive white smoke, and eventually hydrolock or cylinder washing that destroys piston rings and bores.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace all six piezo injectors, seals, and fuel return lines
repairOptions.longevity: 80k-100k miles; viable if caught before cylinder damage occurs

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt engine with new injectors, tested fuel system, and upgraded components
repairOptions.longevity: 12-month warranty; necessary if injector failure caused bore scoring or hydrolock

problems.recommendation: If you notice rough running, misfires on cold start, or white smoke that doesn't clear, get injector deviation values read immediately. Replacing all six injectors proactively at a specialist (£1,200-£1,800) is far cheaper than the engine replacement that follows if one fails catastrophically. On a C6 A6 Allroad worth £3,000-£6,000, full replacement only makes sense if the body and air suspension are in excellent condition.
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt 3.0 TDI ASB engine prices for your A6 Allroad

problems.h4: 2.7T Twin-Turbo Failure - The C5 Allroad Enthusiast’s Nightmare
problems.affectedModels: Audi C5 Allroad 2.7T (C5; 2000-2005, engine codes APB, AZA, BEL)
problems.typicalFailureMileage: 90,000 - 140,000 miles (often sooner on neglected oil changes)
problems.rootCause: The 2.7T’s twin-turbo setup suffers from oil starvation due to clogged oil feed lines, leading to turbo shaft failure, blown seals, and metal contamination throughout the engine. The factory oil sludge problem from long service intervals accelerates this.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace both turbos, oil feed lines, and perform oil system flush
repairOptions.longevity: 40k-60k miles; only viable if metal debris hasn't reached the bottom end

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt engine with upgraded turbos, oil pump, and documented rebuild
repairOptions.longevity: 12-month warranty; necessary if metal contamination has damaged bearings or crank

problems.recommendation: The 2.7T is an enthusiast engine - if you own one, you already know the risks. A specialist rebuild with upgraded turbo oil feed lines and a high-flow oil pump is the only long-term solution. Don't bother with dealer pricing; use an Allroad specialist who knows the 2.7T inside out. On a C5 Allroad worth £3,000-£7,000 (mint examples command more), a full specialist rebuild at £4,000-£5,500 is often worthwhile for a car you intend to keep.
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt 2.7T APB engine prices for your C5 Allroad

problems.h4: 3.0 TDI Rocker Arm Failure - The Hidden C7 BiTDI Catastrophe
problems.affectedModels: Audi A6 Allroad 3.0 TDI, 3.0 BiTDI (C7 facelift; 2014-2018, engine codes CZVA, CRTE)
problems.typicalFailureMileage: 70,000 - 110,000 miles (reported as early as 50k on some CRTE BiTDI units)
problems.rootCause: The roller rocker arms on the EA897 Gen2 engine use small needle bearings that fail prematurely. When a rocker arm collapses, the valve stops opening, the piston strikes the valve, and the engine destroys itself internally - often with metal contamination throughout the oil system.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace all rocker arms, camshafts if worn, and cam bridge with updated components
repairOptions.longevity: 60k-80k miles; only possible if caught before a rocker fails

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt engine with upgraded rocker arms, full oil system flush, and new timing components
repairOptions.longevity: 12-month warranty; only option once a rocker has failed and bent valves

problems.recommendation: Listen for a ticking noise from the top end that changes with engine speed - that's the early warning. If you catch it before failure, a specialist rocker arm replacement costs £1,600-£2,400 and saves the engine. Once a rocker fails, the engine is terminal. On a C7 A6 Allroad BiTDI worth £10,000-£18,000, replacement is absolutely the right choice - just use a specialist who knows to replace all rockers and inspect the camshafts.
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt 3.0 BiTDI CRTE engine prices for your A6 Allroad

problems.h4: 2.0 TDI EGR Cooler Failure - The Silent Killer of B8/B9 A4 Allroad Diesels
problems.affectedModels: Audi A4 Allroad 2.0 TDI (B8, B8.5, B9; 2008-2020, engine codes CAEA, CAGA, CYKB, CYMC)
problems.rootCause: The factory EGR cooler develops internal leaks, allowing coolant to enter the exhaust system. Initially this causes slow coolant loss and white smoke. Left unchecked, it leads to DPF clogging, hydro-lock from coolant ingestion, and finally catastrophic engine failure.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace EGR cooler, coolant flush, and test DPF condition
repairOptions.longevity: 60k-80k miles; viable if caught before DPF or engine damage occurs

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace EGR cooler, DPF, and perform forced regeneration
repairOptions.longevity: 80k-100k miles; necessary if DPF is clogged from coolant contamination

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt engine after hydrolock or piston ring damage from coolant ingestion
repairOptions.longevity: 12-month warranty; necessary if engine has ingested enough coolant to bend rods or score bores

problems.recommendation: Don't ignore coolant loss on a 2.0 TDI A4 Allroad - it's rarely a simple hose leak. If you're topping up coolant every month, get the EGR cooler tested immediately. A specialist EGR cooler replacement at £500-£800 is cheap insurance. On a B8 Allroad worth £4,000-£7,000, full engine replacement only makes sense if the rest of the car is in exceptional condition.
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt 2.0 TDI CAEA engine prices for your A4 Allroad

problems.group: 2.7T / 4.2 V8 Oil Sludge & Cooling System Failure
problems.h4: 2.7T / 4.2 V8 Oil Sludge & Cooling System Failure - C5 Allroad Neglect Damage
problems.affectedModels: Audi C5 Allroad 2.7T, 4.2 V8 (C5; 2000-2005, engine codes APB, AZA, BEL, BAS, AYS)
problems.typicalFailureMileage: 80,000 - 130,000 miles (heavily dependent on oil change history)
problems.rootCause: The C5 Allroad’s long-life service intervals (up to 20,000 miles) cause oil sludge that blocks turbo feed lines (2.7T) and timing chain tensioner passages (4.2 V8). Combined with failing plastic cooling system components, overheating and oil starvation are common.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace coolant expansion tank, rear coolant pipes, thermostat, and water pump
repairOptions.longevity: 40k-60k miles; essential preventative work on any C5 Allroad

repairOptions.tier: Intermediate - Oil System Flush & Turbo Feed Lines
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Remove sump, clean oil pickup, replace turbo feed lines with upgraded units
repairOptions.longevity: 40k-60k miles; viable if turbos/bearings haven't failed yet

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt engine with cleaned oil galleries, upgraded cooling, and documented rebuild history
repairOptions.longevity: 12-month warranty; necessary once sludge has killed bearings or turbos

problems.recommendation: If you buy a C5 Allroad, assume the previous owner ignored the oil changes. A specialist cooling system refresh and oil pickup clean should be your first spend - budget £1,500-£2,000 upfront. On a car worth £3,000-£6,000, that preventative work is what separates a reliable classic from a driveway ornament. Full engine replacement only makes sense for rust-free, low-mileage examples or cars with documented enthusiast ownership.
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt C5 Allroad 2.7T and 4.2 V8 engine prices

finalCta.h4: Don't Let Engine Failure Write Off Your Audi Allroad
finalCta.paragraph: Whether you're facing 2.0 TFSI timing chain rattle, 3.0 TDI injector failure, or a collapsed rocker arm on a BiTDI, the numbers above show one thing clearly: specialist pricing is consistently 30-50% below main dealer costs for the same work. EngineMarket makes it simple to compare prices from trusted UK Audi Allroad specialists who know the quirks of every engine code - from the 2.7T’s oil starvation issues to the C7’s rocker arm weakness. items.title: Audi Allroad 2.5L Diesel Engines
items.description: The 2.5-litre V6 TDI was the sole diesel option for the original C5 Allroad (1999-2005). With 180hp and a distinctive V6 rumble, it offered relaxed touring torque and remains popular in the enthusiast classic market, though low unit numbers make replacement engines relatively scarce.
commonFailurePoints: camshaft wear from extended oil change intervals
items.cta: Get quotes for Audi Allroad 2.5L diesel engines

items.title: Audi Allroad 2.0L Diesel Engines
items.description: The 2.0-litre four-cylinder TDI became the diesel backbone of the A4 Allroad from 2008 onwards. Available in multiple power outputs from 143hp to 190hp across B8, B8.5 and B9 generations, these high-volume units are the most common diesel replacement enquiry for A4 Allroad owners.
commonFailurePoints: EGR cooler failure causing coolant loss
items.cta: Get quotes for Audi Allroad 2.0L diesel engines

items.title: Audi Allroad 3.0L Diesel Engines
items.description: The 3.0-litre V6 TDI is the highest-volume diesel in Allroad history, dominating A6 Allroad search demand. Fitted across C6, C6 facelift, C7 and C7 facelift generations, it evolved from 230hp ASB units to the 320hp BiTDI (CRTE). Owners seek these engines constantly for replacement due to injector and rocker arm failures.
commonFailurePoints: Injector failure on early ASB/BHK; rocker arm failure on CZVA/CRTE BiTDI units
items.cta: Get quotes for Audi Allroad 3.0L diesel engines

items.title: Audi Allroad 2.0L Petrol Engines
items.description: The 2.0-litre TFSI four-cylinder became the petrol mainstay of the A4 Allroad from 2008, offering 211hp in B8/B8.5 form and evolving to 190-252hp with mild hybrid assistance in the B9 generation. Timing chain tensioner failure on pre-2012 units drives a significant proportion of petrol replacement searches.
items.cta: Compare Audi Allroad 2.0L petrol engine prices

items.title: Audi Allroad 2.7L Petrol Engines
items.description: The 2.7-litre twin-turbo V6 (2.7T) is the iconic engine of the original C5 Allroad. Producing 250hp from its twin K03 turbos, it has a passionate enthusiast following despite well-documented reliability issues. Rebuilds with upgraded turbos command premium prices in the specialist market.
items.cta: Compare Audi Allroad 2.7L petrol engine prices

items.title: Audi Allroad 3.0L Petrol Engines
items.description: The 3.0-litre supercharged V6 (3.0 TFSI) brought effortless performance to the C7 A6 Allroad with 310-333hp from a roots-type supercharger. A rare but desirable option in the UK, these engines offer linear power delivery without turbo lag but suffer from specific supercharger and cooling system issues.
items.cta: Compare Audi Allroad 3.0L petrol engine prices

items.title: Audi Allroad 3.2L Petrol Engines
items.description: The 3.2-litre FSI V6 was the range-topping petrol engine for the B8 A4 Allroad before the supercharged 3.0 TFSI arrived. With 265hp from a naturally aspirated V6, it offers a different character to the turbocharged 2.0 TFSI but is relatively rare in UK specification.
items.cta: Compare Audi Allroad 3.2L petrol engine prices

items.title: Audi Allroad 4.2L Petrol Engines
items.description: The 4.2-litre V8 transformed the C5 Allroad into a 300hp luxury express and later appeared in the C6 A6 Allroad with 335hp. These rare, high-performance engines have dedicated collector followings but require meticulous maintenance. Replacement engines are expensive and specialist-only.
compatibleModels: C5 Allroad 4.2 V8 (2003-2005); A6 Allroad 4.2 V8 C6

CYMC / later variant - 2.0 TDI diesel, 150-190hp

Lower engine failure rates than earlier generations

faq.h2: Audi Allroad Engine Replacement - Frequently Asked Questions

items.question: How much does an Audi Allroad engine replacement cost in the UK?
items.answer: A replacement Audi Allroad engine typically costs £1,800-£4,500 for a rebuilt unit supply-only, or £2,300-£5,500 with supply and fit. The 3.0 TDI BiTDI (CRTE) sits at the higher end (£3,000-£5,000), while 2.0 TDI four-cylinder engines are more affordable (£1,200-£2,600).
keyPoints: 2.0 TDI (CAEA/CAGA): £1,200-£2,400 rebuilt supply-only
3.0 TDI (ASB/CDUC): £1,800-£3,200 rebuilt supply-only
3.0 BiTDI (CRTE): £2,400-£4,200 rebuilt supply-only
Specialist fitting adds £500-£1,000 depending on quattro/air suspension
12-month warranty included on all EngineMarket supplied units
items.cta: Compare Audi Allroad engine prices by variant and engine code

items.question: Is the Audi Allroad 3.0 TDI BiTDI reliable? I've heard about rocker arm failure.
items.answer: The CRTE BiTDI is powerful (320hp) but has documented rocker arm failure between 70k-110k miles. A ticking noise from the top end is the early warning - ignore it and the engine will destroy itself. Preventative rocker arm replacement costs £1,600-£2,400 at a specialist.
keyPoints: Rocker arm needle bearings fail prematurely on EA897 Gen2 engines
Ticking noise that changes with engine speed = urgent inspection needed
Once a rocker collapses, valves bend and metal contaminates the oil system
Preventative replacement is available before failure occurs
Budget £4,000-£6,500 for full engine replacement after catastrophic failure
items.cta: Get quotes for CRTE BiTDI rocker arm inspection and replacement

items.question: What are the most common problems with the Audi Allroad 2.0 TFSI engine?
items.answer: The B8/B8.5 2.0 TFSI (CDNC/CGQB) is known for timing chain tensioner failure (pre-2012), water pump leaks, and oil consumption from piston ring wear. Post-2012 tensioners are improved but still fail. A rattling noise on cold startup for 1-3 seconds means the tensioner is failing.
keyPoints: Timing chain tensioner failure can snap the chain and destroy valves
Rattling on cold start = immediate tensioner replacement needed
Water pump/thermostat housing leaks are common (£500-£800 specialist fix)
Oil consumption from worn piston rings affects higher-mileage units
Preventative chain replacement costs £800-£1,400 at a specialist
items.cta: Compare rebuilt 2.0 TFSI engine prices for your A4 Allroad

items.question: Should I replace or repair my Audi Allroad engine after a major failure?
items.answer: On a C5 Allroad worth £3,000-£6,000, full engine replacement (£3,800-£6,000) only makes sense if the body and air suspension are sound. On a C7 A6 Allroad BiTDI worth £10,000-£18,000, replacement is almost always worth it. Specialist pricing is consistently 30-50% below main dealer.
keyPoints: C5 Allroad with high miles or rust: scrap or sell as spares or repair
C6 A6 Allroad with good body: replacement viable if under 150k miles
C7/C8 Allroad: almost always worth replacing due to high residual value
Rebuilt engine with upgraded components often outlasts the original
Get a specialist inspection before committing to either path
items.cta: Get honest advice on Audi Allroad repair vs replacement for your car

items.question: How much does a used Audi Allroad engine cost vs a reconditioned one?
items.answer: Used engines from breakers cost £900-£2,200 but carry significant risk - hidden wear, poor storage, and no meaningful warranty. Reconditioned units cost £1,800-£4,200 but include new wear parts, bench testing, and a 12-24 month warranty. The upfront saving on used often disappears when it fails months later.
keyPoints: Used engines: no warranty or 30-90 days only - buyer beware
Reconditioned: factory-spec rebuild with new pistons, rings, bearings
Rebuilt (specialist): upgraded components addressing original failure points
Remanufactured: fully tested to exceed OEM standards, 24-month warranty
Always ask suppliers what "reconditioned" actually means before buying
items.cta: Compare used, rebuilt, and reconditioned Audi Allroad engine prices

items.question: What engine codes were fitted to the Audi Allroad C5, C6 and C7 generations?
items.answer: C5 (1999-2005): 2.7T (APB/AZA/BEL), 2.5 TDI (AKE/BAU), 4.2 V8 (BAS/AYS). C6 A6 Allroad (2006-2011): 3.0 TDI (ASB/BHK), 4.2 V8 (AUK), 3.2 FSI (BVK). C7 A6 Allroad (2012-2018): 3.0 TDI (CDUC/CDYB/CZVA/CRTE), 3.0 TFSI supercharged (CGXB/CRE).
keyPoints: B8/B8.5 A4 Allroad (2008-2016): 2.0 TDI (CAEA/CAGA/CYKB/CYMC), 2.0 TFSI (CDNC/CGQB)
B9 A4 Allroad (2016-2020): 2.0 TFSI (DETA) with 12V MHEV, 2.0 TDI (CYMC)
C8 A6 Allroad (2019+): 3.0 TDI with 48V MHEV (DGV/DHP variants)

Each code has specific failure patterns - know yours before buying

items.question: How long does an Audi Allroad engine typically last before needing replacement?
items.answer: Well-maintained 3.0 TDI V6 engines regularly reach 150k-200k miles. The 2.0 TDI four-cylinder typically lasts 120k-160k miles. 2.0 TFSI petrol engines often need chain tensioner attention by 100k miles. The 2.7T twin-turbo can reach high mileage but requires meticulous oil change history and upgraded turbo lines.
keyPoints: ASB/BHK 3.0 TDI: injector failure common 80k-130k miles - replace injectors proactively
CRTE BiTDI: rocker arm risk 70k-110k miles - early replacement saves engine
2.0 TFSI (pre-2012): timing chain tensioner failure risk from 60k miles
Regular oil changes (every 8k-10k miles, not Audi's long-life) extend life significantly
Cooling system neglect kills more Allroad engines than internal failures

items.question: Can I fit an Audi Allroad engine myself or do I need a specialist?
items.answer: Do not attempt DIY. Allroad engines require quattro drivetrain integration, pneumatic air suspension clearance, and specific oil pan configurations for the prop shaft. A general mechanic will struggle - you need an Audi Allroad specialist who understands these complexities.
keyPoints: Quattro system requires correct oil pan and prop shaft alignment
Air suspension components are easily damaged during engine removal
Engine codes must match exactly - wrong ECU mapping causes immediate issues
Specialist fitting costs £500-£1,000 but saves thousands in rectification
Main dealer rates are 30-50% higher than independent specialists
items.cta: Find vetted UK Audi Allroad specialists for supply and fit

items.question: Is a reconditioned Audi Allroad engine as good as a new one?
items.answer: A properly reconditioned or remanufactured engine is as reliable as new - often better, because specialists upgrade known weak points (e.g., improved timing chain tensioners, revised rocker arms). Remanufactured units are fully tested to exceed factory tolerances. New dealer engines cost £8,000-£12,000; reconditioned costs £1,800-£4,200.
keyPoints: Reconditioned: stripped, inspected, machined, new wear parts fitted
Remanufactured: non-destructive testing of block/head, exceeds OEM spec
Upgraded components address original failure patterns (e.g., revised rockers on CRTE)
12-24 month warranty included on all EngineMarket supplied units
Half the price of a dealer new engine with comparable reliability
items.cta: Compare reconditioned Audi Allroad engines with warranty from UK specialists

items.question: Which Audi Allroad engine is the most reliable and should I buy used?
items.answer: The 3.0 TDI CDUC/CDYB (C7, 204-245hp) is the most reliable Allroad diesel - fewer injector issues than ASB and no rocker arm problems like the CRTE BiTDI. For petrol, the 3.0 TFSI supercharged (CGXB/CRE) is solid. Avoid buying used engines without documented service history and compression test results.
keyPoints: CDUC/CDYB: improved injector design, fewer failures than early 3.0 TDIs
3.0 TFSI supercharged: robust engine, supercharger clutch is the main failure point
2.0 TFSI post-2012: improved timing chain tensioner - safer buy than pre-2012
CRTE BiTDI: high performance but rocker arm risk - budget for preventative work
Used engines: only buy from suppliers offering compression test results and mileage verification
